content:
# Exodus 25 — Disagreement Log

**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Chapter:** Exodus 25 (40 verses)
**Date:** 2026-03-04

---

## Summary

Of 40 verses, 18 involved substantive disagreements requiring adjudication. The remaining 22 verses achieved near-unanimous agreement across all three translators. The major areas of disagreement concerned: (1) the rendering of key technical terms (*terumah*, *takhash*, *zer*, *kaftorim*, *makhtotot*, *misgeret*); (2) the degree of dynamic restructuring permissible in prose instruction; and (3) PNEUMA's tendency to paraphrase Hebrew constructions, add clarifying words, and restructure clause order.

---

## Verse-by-Verse Disagreements

### v. 1 — *le'mor* ("saying")
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "saying" |
| LOGOS | "saying" |
| PNEUMA | *(omitted)* |
| **SOPHIA** | **"saying"** |
| **Reason** | PNEUMA drops *le'mor* entirely. This particle marks the formal speech-introduction formula and must be preserved. EMET/LOGOS followed. |

---

### v. 2 — *terumah* ("contribution" vs. "offering")
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "contribution" |
| LOGOS | "offering" |
| PNEUMA | "offering" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"contribution"** |
| **Reason** | *Terumah* (from *rum*, "to lift up") denotes something voluntarily "raised up" and set apart. "Offering" is too generic and conflates *terumah* with sacrificial terms (*qorban*, *zevakh*). EMET's "contribution" preserves the distinction. |

---

### v. 2 — *dabber* ("speak" vs. "tell")
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "Speak" |
| LOGOS | "Speak" |
| PNEUMA | "Tell" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"Speak"** |
| **Reason** | The piel imperative *dabber* carries formal, authoritative register. "Tell" (PNEUMA) diminishes this to casual instruction. |

---

### v. 2 — *veyiqchu* ("let them take" vs. "to bring")
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "let them take" |
| LOGOS | "let them take" |
| PNEUMA | "to bring" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"let them take"** |
| **Reason** | The verb is *laqakh* ("to take"), not *bo* hiphil ("to bring"). PNEUMA reverses the verb's direction. The Hebrew frames the act from the perspective of the collectors, and may imply a return of what already belongs to God. |

---

### v. 2 — *yiddevennu* (singular vs. plural)
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "whose heart moves him" |
| LOGOS | "whose heart moves him" |
| PNEUMA | "whose heart moves them to give" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"whose heart moves him"** |
| **Reason** | The Hebrew is singular (*kol-ish...yiddevennu libbo*, "every man...his heart moves him"). PNEUMA shifts to plural "them" and adds "to give," which is not in the text. |

---

### v. 5 — *orot tekhashim* ("takhash skins" vs. "fine leather")
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "skins of takhash" |
| LOGOS | "tahash skins" |
| PNEUMA | "fine leather" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"takhash skins"** |
| **Reason** | The identity of *takhash* is genuinely unknown. PNEUMA's "fine leather" imports an interpretation not warranted by the Hebrew. The term is left transliterated, following the convention applied to other uncertain terms (*ephod*, *cherubim*). |

---

### v. 6 — *lamma'or* ("the light" vs. "the lamps")
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "the light" |
| LOGOS | "the light" |
| PNEUMA | "the lamps" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"the light"** |
| **Reason** | The Hebrew *ma'or* is singular ("luminary, light"). PNEUMA's plural "lamps" anticipates the lampstand description not yet given. |

---

### v. 8 — *ve'asu* ("let them make" vs. "let them build")
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "let them make" |
| LOGOS | "let them make" |
| PNEUMA | "let them build" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"let them make"** |
| **Reason** | *Asah* ("to make, do") is broader than "build" (*banah*). The tabernacle involves textile work, metalwork, and woodwork -- not merely construction. |

---

### v. 8 — *veshakhanti betokham* ("and I will dwell" vs. "so that I may dwell")
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "and I will dwell in their midst" |
| LOGOS | "and I will dwell in their midst" |
| PNEUMA | "so that I may dwell among them" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"and I will dwell in their midst"** |
| **Reason** | The Hebrew uses a *vav*-consecutive perfect (*veshakhanti*), not a purpose clause. PNEUMA's "so that I may dwell" imports a subordination not present in the grammar. The Hebrew presents two coordinate clauses: they will make, and he will dwell. |

---

### v. 9 — *mishkan* ("tabernacle" vs. "dwelling place")
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "tabernacle" |
| LOGOS | "Tabernacle" |
| PNEUMA | "dwelling place" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"tabernacle"** |
| **Reason** | *Mishkan* is the standard technical term for the portable sanctuary. PNEUMA's "dwelling place" dissolves the terminus technicus into a generic phrase. |

---

### v. 9 — *kelav* ("vessels" vs. "furnishings")
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "vessels" |
| LOGOS | "vessels" |
| PNEUMA | "furnishings" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"vessels"** |
| **Reason** | *Kelim* (from *keli*, "vessel, utensil, implement") denotes moveable objects and utensils, not architectural "furnishings." |

---

### v. 11 — *zer* ("molding" vs. "rim")
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "molding" |
| LOGOS | "rim" |
| PNEUMA | "molding" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"molding"** |
| **Reason** | *Zer* denotes a decorative border or crown-molding around the top edge -- an ornamental feature, not merely a structural rim. "Molding" captures the craftsmanship. Applies also to vv. 24, 25. |

---

### v. 19 — *miqqatseh mizzeh...miqqatseh mizzeh* ("from this end...from that end")
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "from this end...from this end" |
| LOGOS | "from this end...from that end" |
| PNEUMA | "at one end...at the other end" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"from this end...from that end"** |
| **Reason** | EMET's literal repetition of "from this" is awkward in English. LOGOS's "from this...from that" is the best rendering. PNEUMA's "at" loses the partitive *min* ("from"), which indicates the cherubim are formed out of the mercy seat itself. |

---

### v. 20 — *sokhekhim* ("covering" vs. "sheltering")
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "covering" |
| LOGOS | "covering" |
| PNEUMA | "sheltering" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"covering"** |
| **Reason** | While *sakhakh* can mean "shelter" or "screen," "covering" is the more precise rendering in context -- the wings cover the mercy seat. "Sheltering" imports a protective connotation beyond the Hebrew's scope in this verse. |

---

### v. 25 — *misgeret* ("border" vs. "frame" vs. "rim")
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "border" |
| LOGOS | "frame" |
| PNEUMA | "rim" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"border"** |
| **Reason** | *Misgeret* (from *sagar*, "to close, shut") denotes an enclosing raised edge. "Frame" (LOGOS) implies a structural element too large; "rim" (PNEUMA) is too thin. "Border" captures the intermediate sense of an enclosing, handbreadth-wide edge. |

---

### v. 29 — Vessel terminology (*qe'arot*, *kappot*, *qesot*, *menaqiyyot*)
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "dishes...ladles...jars...bowls" |
| LOGOS | "plates...ladles...pitchers...bowls" |
| PNEUMA | "plates and dishes...pitchers and bowls" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"dishes...ladles...jars...bowls"** |
| **Reason** | EMET preserves four distinct terms matching the four Hebrew nouns. LOGOS's "plates" and "pitchers" are less precise. PNEUMA conflates the four vessels into two pairs, losing the distinct enumeration of the Hebrew list. |

---

### v. 31 — *kaftorim* ("knobs" vs. "buds")
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "knobs" |
| LOGOS | "buds" |
| PNEUMA | "buds" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"knobs"** |
| **Reason** | *Kaftorim* denotes bulbous ornamental protrusions. "Buds" imports a botanical interpretation that, while plausible in context, narrows the architectural/ornamental semantic range. The term appears in both botanical and non-botanical contexts in the Hebrew Bible. Applies to vv. 31-36. |

---

### v. 37 — *vehe'ir al-ever paneyha* ("upon the space before it")
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "upon the space in front of it" |
| LOGOS | "upon the area before it" |
| PNEUMA | "to the space in front of it" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"upon the space before it"** |
| **Reason** | The preposition is *al* ("upon"), not *le-* or *el-*. PNEUMA's "to" changes the directional sense. EMET's "in front of" and LOGOS's "before" are both acceptable; "before it" is slightly more concise. |

---

### v. 38 — *makhtotot* ("firepans" vs. "trays")
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "firepans" |
| LOGOS | "fire pans" |
| PNEUMA | "trays" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"firepans"** |
| **Reason** | *Makhtotot* (from *khatah*, "to snatch up coals") are specifically fire-related pans for removing ash and charred wick material. PNEUMA's "trays" is too generic and loses the etymological connection to fire. |

---

### v. 40 — *mar'eh* ("being shown" vs. "were shown")
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "are being shown" |
| LOGOS | "are being shown" |
| PNEUMA | "you were shown" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"you are being shown"** |
| **Reason** | The hophal participle *mar'eh* denotes ongoing action -- Moses is presently "being caused to see." PNEUMA's past tense "were shown" implies the showing is complete, losing the participial sense of continuous divine revelation. |

---

## Pattern Summary

| Pattern | Frequency | Examples |
|---|---|---|
| PNEUMA adds words not in Hebrew | 4 | v. 2 "to give"; v. 8 "so that"; v. 9 restructuring; v. 2 "tell" |
| PNEUMA uses dynamic paraphrase | 6 | v. 5 "fine leather"; v. 8 "build"; v. 9 "dwelling place"; v. 9 "furnishings"; v. 29 conflation; v. 40 past tense |
| LOGOS vs. EMET on technical terms | 3 | *zer* (rim/molding); *misgeret* (frame/border); *kaftorim* (buds/knobs) |
| EMET hyper-literal phrasing | 1 | v. 19 "from this...from this" |
| All three agree | 22 | vv. 3, 10, 12, 13, 15, 16, 17, 23, 24, 26, 27, 28, 30, 32, 33, 34, 35, 36, 39, and partially on vv. 14, 18, 21 |

---

## Consensus Statistics

- **Total verses:** 40
- **Unanimous agreement:** 22 verses (55%)
- **EMET followed:** 15 times (primary)
- **LOGOS followed:** 3 times (primary)
- **PNEUMA followed:** 0 times (primary)
- **EMET/LOGOS joint followed:** 14 times
- **Disagreements logged:** 18

---

*Compiled by SOPHIA from EMET (Literal), LOGOS (Formal), and PNEUMA (Dynamic) translations.*
